title.value: Some Typical Advances in the Synthetic Applications of Allenes

abstract.value: Allenes are a class of unique compounds with two c0-orbitals perpendicular to each other. The first synthesis can be traced back to 1887. They also exist in many natural products with interesting biological activities. The development of IR and Raman spectroscopy promoted the development of allene chemistry greatly. For a long period of time, allenes were considered as highly unstable, which retarded the development of the chemistry of allenes. However, during the last 8-10 years, allenes have been shown to demonstrate nice reactivities as well as selectivities, which can usually be tuned by the electronic or steric effects and the nature of the catalysts involved. This review will summarize some of the most typical advances on the chemistry of allenes, excluding those having been already included in the related reviews. In some cases, to keep the chemistry complete, there may be some minor overlap with the contents in published reviews or books.

warnings: No explicit abstract was visible in the supplied snippets; the abstract field uses the introductory summary paragraph.
This paper is a broad review, so the structured tags are generalized across multiple allene reaction classes.
